name: HCSS Heavy Construction Systems Software API
what_it_does: HCSS REST API for heavy construction estimating, bidding, field operations, and equipment management. Enables AI agents to manage bid and estimate creation for heavy civil construction projects, handle field time and labor tracking for construction payroll and job costing, access equipment telematics and maintenance workflow for construction fleet management, retrieve safety observation and incident reporting for construction safety programs, manage daily field reports and production tracking for construction project documentation, handle subcontractor management and insurance tracking for procurement compliance, access fuel management and equipment cost tracking for fleet cost management, retrieve project schedule and production benchmarking for heavy civil performance, manage truck ticketing and material quantity tracking for earthwork and paving operations, and integrate HCSS data with Procore, ERP, and construction technology platforms.
use_cases: Automating bids from construction estimating agents
Tracking field production from heavy civil agents
Managing equipment from construction fleet agents
Reporting safety incidents from construction safety agents
not_for: Vertical building construction without heavy civil/earthwork focus
Small contractors without enterprise heavy civil scale
Real estate development without construction field operations
best_when: A heavy civil contractor (highway, utility, earthwork, paving) using HCSS wants AI agents to automate estimating, field production tracking, equipment management, and safety reporting.
avoid_when: BIDDING RISK: HCSS estimate automation affects public bid submissions — automated bid generation without estimator review creates underbid risk on public contracts. Davis-Bacon and prevailing wage automation must validate craft classifications for public heavy civil work. HCSS API documentation is partner-facing; integration typically requires HCSS professional services.

auth.notes: HCSS uses OAuth 2.0. API portal at hcss.com/api. Webhooks for construction events. REST API with JSON. Sugar Land, Texas HQ. Private (employee-owned). Founded 1986. 4,000+ heavy civil contractor customers. HCSS HeavyBid (estimating), HeavyJob (field), Equipment360, Safety. Competes with B2W Software and ARES PRISM for heavy civil contractor software.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription per module
pricing.notes: Sugar Land, Texas. HCSS founded 1986. Employee-owned private company. 4,000+ heavy civil customers. HeavyBid, HeavyJob, Equipment360 modules. Competes with B2W Software and Penta Technologies for heavy civil.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per integration

known_agent_gotchas: BIDDING RISK: Automated bid generation for public projects requires estimator review; underbid on public contracts creates financial loss on fixed-price government work
Davis-Bacon prevailing wage — public heavy civil work has federal and state prevailing wage requirements; automated labor classification without craft code validation creates wage violation
Equipment telematics data lag — equipment GPS ping intervals vary (5-15 min); automated fuel and equipment cost calculations must account for GPS data latency

Truck ticket reconciliation — automated material quantity tracking via truck tickets must match load tickets to delivery tickets; automated reconciliation without driver confirmation creates quantity disputes
HCSS module-specific APIs — HeavyBid (estimating) and HeavyJob (field) have different API surfaces; verify which module integration path is required
Safety incident reporting — OSHA 300 log automation requires careful injury/illness classification; automated incident classification without safety officer review creates OSHA recordkeeping errors
agent_readiness.security_notes: Heavy construction software. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Construction bid, field, and equipment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Heavy construction. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Bid, field, and equipment data.
